Tutorial - GMS2 Top Down Shooter

If you want to see tutorial in a separate web browser window click here.

Audio

Pick some free music that you think might be appropriate for the game. I used The Spririt of Russion Love by Zinaida Troikai for mine from the free music archive. Look for an MP4 or OGG format. Download a song that you have permission to use and right click on Sounds and select Create Sound. Call it snd_theme_music and press the three periods (dots) next to the name to bring up an explorer menu to pick the music.

Pick the music you have downloaded:

Open obj_game_controller: Create event script and at the bottom check to make sure the song is not already playing, then play the song in an endless loop.

obj_game_controller: Create Event

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. Play it and notice that the music should play and go on forever.

Create another new sound resource and find the file in Included Files | TutorialResources | Sounds | snd_enemy_explode.wav in the browser (or click here). Call this Resource Sound file snd_enemy_explode.

Add the enemy explosion to obj_player_bullet: Collision | obj_enemy_parent event and add to the bottom:

obj_player_bullet: Collision Event

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. Play it and shoot enemies ot hear the sounds played!

Repeat the process for snd_player_explode, snd_player_shoot and snd_enemy_shoot in in Included Files | TutorialResources | Sounds. If you can't find it check out here) and link audio files with the appropriate name.

Lets play all of these sounds in their appropriate places:

obj_enemy_bullet: Collision | obj_player

obj_player: Step

obj_enemy_target: Step

obj_enemy_shoot: Step

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. Play it and listen to all the sounds.

The audio gets repetitive really quickly. Lets look at a way of tricking the ear to get more out of a single sound. Lets alter the pitch with audio_sound_pitch(index, pitch). The pitch is a value less than 1 is lower in pitch (0 would be none) and 2 would be an ocatve higher.

Lets alter the volume (gain) with audio_sound_gain(index, volume, time). The volume argument is between 0 (quiet) to 1 (full volume). We will set the time argument to 0 as we don't want it to fade in or out we want it to apply right away.

Here is an example of what it looks like in obj_player: Step. Repeat this for all other sounds played.

obj_player: Step Event

Run the game by pressing the Play Button. Play it and the sounds should alter a bit during playback.

Next up we will look at creating a front end.
